title: Texas rookie gets mound visit, told he made team
content: The mound visit Carter Baumler received from Rangers manager Skip Schumaker on Monday night wasn't to take the rookie right-hander out of his last spring training game, but rather to let him know he made the Opening Day roster.

title: Warriors' Moody stretchered off with knee injury
content: Warriors wing Moses Moody suffered a noncontact left knee injury late in Monday night's overtime win in Dallas, needing the assistance of a stretcher to exit the floor.

title: Betts erupts for career-high 35 as UCLA rolls on
content: UCLA star Lauren Betts had a career-high 25 points to lead the way in an 87-68 blowout win over Oklahoma State in the second round of the NCAA tournament.

title: Bucks waive guard Thomas to convert Nance
content: The Bucks waived guard Cam Thomas to convert the contract of forward Pete Nance into a standard deal.

title: Cuse women's HC blasts NCAA for UConn pairing
content: Syracuse coach Felisha Legette-Jack blasted the NCAA women's basketball committee for consistently putting her teams in the UConn subregional in Storrs following the Orange's 98-45 second-round loss to the Huskies.

title: Centre mulls delimitation based on 2011 Census to fast-track Women’s Reservation Act
content: “Under the amendments that the government wants to bring in, the basis for the selection of seats will be the Census data of 2011. The number of seats in Parliament will increase to 816, out of which 273 will be reserved for women,” a source said

title: ‘Dhurandhar: The Revenge’ and the perils of ‘nationalist’ violence
content: Such films are enabling the construction of a new kind of Indian citizen, in which a narrowly defined nationalism is the only virtue and is also indissolubly associated with violence

title: Karnataka’s draft policy on ‘Responsible Digital Use Among Students’ sets goals for parents, schools
content: The policy recommended that schools incorporate digital well-being and social media literacy in their curriculum

title: Telangana Cabinet clears Hate Speech and Parents Support Bills
content: Chief Minister keeps his promise on bringing in a law to deduct from salary of employees who neglect their aged parents

title: How Assam has voted since 2011
content: Over the past fifteen years, the state has witnessed dramatic political shifts, especially in 2011, when the BJP won a north-eastern state for the first time.

title: Delhi sees slightly cooler morning at 16.6°C; daytime temperature likely to cross 30°C
content: Delhi's air quality was recorded in the "moderate" category with an AQI of 168, according to data from the Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB).

title: Assembly Elections LIVE updates: ECI approves transfer of returning officers for West Bengal polls
content: A total of 445 candidates have filed nominations for the 30 assembly constituencies in the Union Territory of Puducherry

title: Two more Indian vessels cross Strait of Hormuz
content: Two foreign-owned and foreign-flagged LPG carriers, Hellas Gladiator and Gas Jupiter, each carrying 24,000 tonnes are expected to reach Vishakhapatnam on March 30, 2026.

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: BJP seal on Election Commission of India letter sparks row in State
content: CPI(M) asks whether seals are being casually swapped and all pretences are being dropped. Office of CEO, Kerala, says the 2019 letter, on which the BJP had affixed a seal when its functionaries approached the CEO’s office for clarification, was distributed recently by oversight

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: CPI(M), Congress accuse each other of fragmenting ‘LDF-UDF duopoly’ in Kerala by aiding BJP
content: Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan accuses Congress national leadership under Rahul Gandhi of playing as BJP’s ‘B-Team’. AICC general secretary K.C. Venugopal says PM Modi and Amit Shah have found ‘a willing tool’ in Pinarayi Vijayan to attack Rahul Gandhi, who is ‘fighting a lone battle against Sangh Parivar and its corporate backers’

title: Rebuff to ‘self-styled Vishwaguru’, says Congress, on reports of Pakistan mediating in West Asia
content: Congress said that in spite of India’s undoubted military successes in Operation Sindoor, Pakistan’s diplomatic engagement and narrative management has been “markedly superior to that of the Modi government”

title: Palaniswami assails Stalin for indulging in ‘disinformation’ campaign on NDA leaders’ visits to New Delhi
content: The visits were meant for holding discussions on the Assembly election, and for “exchange of views”, the AIADMK leader says

title: Karnataka bypolls: CM, DyCM, Union Ministers accompany party nominees; 58 in all file nominations
content: In all, 19 candidates filed nomination papers for the Bagalkot seat, while 39 candidates filed their nominations for the Davangere South seat before the deadline ended on Monday (March 23).

title: Kerala polls: CPI(M) slams UDF’s Ramesh Pisharody over Gaza stance; Thomas Isaac’s ‘joker’ jibe sparks row
content: CPI(M) raises questions about ‘apolitical’ nature of actor-politician Ramesh Pisharody, the UDF candidate in Palakkad, pointing at his earlier statements dismissing protests in Kerala against deaths in Gaza

title: A.P. to launch digital system for streamlined medical bill reimbursements
content: According to a release, new software will be developed by the A.P. Centre for Financial Systems and Services (APCFSS), keeping in mind the current system.

title: 25 leopards relocated from Maharashtra to Gujarat's Vantara facility: Minister
content: The population of leopards has risen four times in 10 years. The State, which had 101 tigers seven-eight years back, now has 444, Maharashtra’s Forest Minister Ganesh Naik said

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election: CPI (M) accepts five-seat offer, signs pact with DMK
content: The CPI (M), which was allotted six seats in the last Assembly elections in the same alliance, sought double-digit seats from the DMK but was denied due to the entry of more parties in the alliance

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: LDF seeks hat-trick, UDF eyes comeback in Mananthavady
content: Adding a new dimension to the campaign, a coalition of Adivasi organisations under the banner of the Adivasi Gothra Maha Sabha has come up with their disgruntlement over the reluctance of leading fronts to field a candidate from the Paniya community

title: Bill to amend trans rights law listed for consideration, passing in Lok Sabha on March 24
content: Bill proposes amendments on transgender individuals, the procedure for obtaining a transgender certificate, and compliance requirements for medical institutions that may perform gender-affirming care procedures

title: Ahead of Chandrayaan-4, IIT and PRL team decodes moon’s titanium-rich rocks
content: The Chandrayaan-4 mission, which ISRO has planned for 2028, aims to collect rock samples from the moon and return them to the earth, making the choice of landing site critical; the study’s findings could help inform that decision

title: Nitish Kumar kicks off fifth phase of Samriddhi Yatra
content: The Chief Minister announced that his government has set a target of providing jobs and employment opportunities to 1 crore youths in the next five years

title: Packaged water turns costly as raw material prices rise due to West Asia conflict
content: The cost of each cap used in bottles has increased from about 10 paise to 30 paise or 40 paise; the price of a 20-litre bubble-top container has been increased by ₹5 to offset rising input cost

title: Transgender persons hold protests demanding withdrawal of amendment to Transgender Bill
content: Transgender rights activists noted that these amendments violate the fundamental rights guaranteed under the Indian Constitution and contradicts the historic 2014 NALSA judgment by the Supreme Court

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: 25,03,842 voters in final list in Ernakulam; number goes up by 34,048 post SIR
content: According to the latest list, there are 12,18,707 men, 12,85,108 women, and 27 transgender people in the  district

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: 120 candidates file nominations in Ernakulam as deadline ends
content: Those who submitted the nominations include official candidates announced by political parties, dummy candidates put up by parties, and Independents

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election 2026: Ramadoss, Sasikala join hands, to field candidates in all 234 seats
content: S. Ramadoss did not clarify under which banner his candidates would be fielded as the judiciary has refused to restrain his estranged son Anbumani from using the name and flag of the PMK

title: A true benevolent authoritarian enforces discipline for the greater good: Seeman
content: Naam Tamilar Katchi chief coordinator Seeman contends that without discipline, there will be no order; he bats for an economy centred on agriculture in T.N. and plans to create government-led industries based on local resources if his party emerges victorious in the Assembly election

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election 2026: NTK releases manifesto; pitches decentralised governance, anti-drug measures, Tamil-centric policies
content: The manifesto proposes a major administrative overhaul, including the creation of multiple functional capitals across Tamil Nadu to ease pressure on Chennai and improve access to governance

title: People will assess governance failure, corruption and favour UDF: KPCC chief
content: Sunny Joseph dismisses concerns of internal dissent within the Congress over granting electoral berth to former CPI(M) leaders; he says the UDF’s decision to support such leaders will strengthen the coalition; results of local body elections indicate strong anti-incumbency sentiment, he adds

title: Medical bodies raise concerns over permits for procedures
content: Warn of safety risks in Dental Council of India nod to Master of Dental Surgery doctors to perform such surgeries

title: Supreme Court gives Centre four-week leeway to respond to petition on airfare hike, says govt. handling ‘bigger problems now’
content: Petitioner had sought steps to address fare fluctuation, fare transparency, and continuity of essential air services

content: Solicitor General Tushar Mehta promises to personally convey the court’s message to ED and CBI directors in “harsher terms”

title: Impact of West Asia war likely to be felt for long time, PM tells Lok Sabha

title: ED restitutes $5.6 billion assets in corruption cases: Director

title: Do not villainise subsidies as they strengthen our economy: Krishna Byre Gowda
content: Boosting consumer demand by providing money to poor people in the form of subsidies is a sure-shot approach for economic growth wherever there is an economic recession, he tells the Legislative Assembly

title: Did someone know? $580 million oil bet hit seconds before Trump’s Iran update
content: The timing of these trades mirrors earlier instances in recent months where large and highly profitable bets were placed on prediction platform Polymarket ahead of US military actions involving Iran and Venezuela.

title: 'Will blow up building': Delhi assembly gets bomb threat ahead of Budget session
content: A bomb threat email targeted the Delhi Assembly Speaker's office, warning of explosions at the Assembly building and the Vidhan Sabha Metro station. The message also named prominent political figures including Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Union Home Minister Amit Shah.

title: Google CEO Sundar Pichai's one-line reaction to ChatGPT launch
content: Google CEO Sundar Pichai revealed his "uncomfortably exciting" reaction to ChatGPT's 2022 launch, admitting the speed of AI diffusion surprised even his "AI-first" company. This prompted a 'Code Red' that ultimately led to the development of Gemini 3, now outperforming rivals. Pichai emphasized Google's quiet, full-stack innovation approach, building necessary infrastructure before product release.

title: ‘Dhurandhar: The Revenge’ box office collection: Film crosses Rs 800 crore mark
content: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' is a massive Bollywood blockbuster, crossing Rs 500 crore net domestically and Rs 800 crore worldwide. The spy thriller, a sequel to the 2025 hit, continues its successful box office run despite a slight dip on its first Monday. The film's gripping narrative and strong performances are driving its phenomenal success.

title: Kejriwal’s bungalow facelift cost Rs 33.6 crore, 342% above estimate: CAG report
content: A CAG report revealed Arvind Kejriwal's former Delhi residence renovation cost ₹33.6 crore, significantly exceeding estimates. Separately, a PAC report indicated the withdrawn 2021-22 excise policy led to a ₹2,002 crore loss for Delhi, with Manish Sisodia's role questioned.  These findings emerge amidst ongoing legal proceedings.

title: H-1B registration dips 30-50%: Companies that are emerging as ‘favourites’ in new system
content: H-1B visa registrations for FY27 have plummeted by 30-50%, driven by higher fees and a new lottery prioritizing wages. This shift benefits mid-tier IT firms and US Master's graduates, while large outsourcing companies face reduced dominance. The recalibration signals a move towards quality talent and increased costs for employers.

title: Gold price prediction today: Pressure on gold prices to continue amid US-Iran war?
content: Gold price prediction today: Gold prices are likely to remain range-bound in the near future, says Praveen Singh, Head Currencies and Commodities, Mirae Asset ShareKhan

title: Memorial plaque, 11 seats to honour Chinnaswamy stampede victims: RCB, KSCA
content: M Chinnaswamy Stadium will honor 11 fans lost in a tragedy. A memorial plaque and 11 reserved seats will be unveiled. Royal Challengers Bengaluru will lead tributes before the match. The stadium remembers the lives lost during celebrations. This tribute ensures their memory remains part of the venue.

title: Rajasthan board class 10 result 2026: Check RBSE scores on TOI portal
content: The Board of Secondary Education, Rajasthan (BSER) will announce the RBSE Class 10 results today, March 24, 2026, at 1 PM. Over 10 lakh students who appeared for the exams conducted from February 12 to February 28, 2026, can check their scores on official websites and the Times of India portal.

title: First human patient with Elon Musk's Neuralink brain chip shares 100 days update
content: Noland Arbaugh, the first recipient of Neuralink's N1 implant, celebrated 100 days with the device. Paralyzed since 2016, he now controls a MacBook and plays World of Warcraft using only his thoughts. Arbaugh describes the experience as life-changing, enabling a new level of independence and interaction.

title: 4-month foetus recovered from fridge in Kerala, couple claims miscarriage
content: Police in Thrissur, Kerala, discovered a preserved foetus in a refrigerator at a Kodungallur apartment, leading to the questioning of a married couple. The 23-year-old man and 28-year-old woman claimed the foetus was expelled at home, and they preserved it due to an inability to dispose of it. An investigation is ongoing.

title: A call before strikes: How Netanyahu convinced Trump of Khamenei's joint killing
content: Responding to Reuters, White House spokeswoman Anna Kelly did not directly comment on the call between Trump and Netanyahu but said the operation aimed to "destroy the Iranian regime's ballistic missile and production capacity, annihilate the Iranian regime's Navy, end their ability to arm proxies, and guarantee that Iran can never obtain a nuclear weapon." There was no immediate response from Netanyahu’s office or Iran’s UN representative to requests for comment.

title: Stock market today: Sensex jumps over 1,100 points; Nifty50 opens above 22,800
content: Stock market today: Indian equity benchmarks Nifty50 and BSE Sensex rallied in opening trade on Tuesday after a day of massive selloff. Hopes of an early halt to the US-Iran war led to the stock market rebounding.

title: Kim Jong Un calls Pyongyang's nuclear status irreversible, warns against 'most hostile' South Korea
content: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un has said his country will not give up its nuclear weapons and will continue with a hard-line stance, calling South Korea its “most hostile” state and accusing the United States of “state terrorism.” He signalled readiness for either confrontation or dialogue, while talks with Washington remain stalled.

title: 'Muslims not allowed': Delhi man claims ride denied due to religion
content: A Delhi commuter was allegedly denied a cab ride by a Rapido driver who explicitly stated Muslims were not allowed. The incident, captured in an audio clip shared online, has sparked outrage and prompted Rapido to suspend the driver. The company has reiterated its zero-tolerance policy against discrimination.

title: Horrific murder caught on cam in Bengaluru: Man bludgeoned to death with jack rod
content: A 27-year-old man was brutally murdered near Bhovi Colony in Bengaluru after an altercation over money. The accused, an auto driver and his friend, were arrested within six hours. The victim had allegedly been harassing the accused for funds to support his drinking habits, leading to the fatal confrontation.

title: Oil prices today: Crude nears $104 as Middle East tensions persist
content: Global oil prices rebounded Tuesday amid escalating Middle East tensions and diplomatic uncertainty. Brent crude neared $104 a barrel as the Strait of Hormuz disruptions continue to tighten supply, with Gulf producers cutting output. Analysts warn of prolonged tightness and potential demand destruction if the situation persists, keeping markets volatile.

title: Iftar party on boat in Varanasi: Court denies bail to 14 arrested
content: A Varanasi court denied bail to 14 individuals arrested for an alleged Iftar gathering on the Ganga, accused of eating chicken biryani on a boat. Charges include defiling a place of worship and hurting religious sentiments. The court deemed the offenses grave, citing the prosecution's argument that granting bail could impede the judicial process.

title: 'Afghan fans 'committed suicide' after Asia Cup loss to Pakistan'
content: Afghanistan opener Rahmanullah Gurbaz said he still finds it difficult to accept how his team lost that match at the Sharjah Cricket Stadium.

title: Military plane carrying troops crashes in Colombia: Death toll climbs to 66, many injured
content: A Colombian Air Force Hercules C-130 plane crash near the Peruvian border has claimed 66 lives, with many more injured. The aircraft reportedly struck a tree during takeoff, leading to a devastating fire and detonation of onboard explosives. Local residents were the first responders, aiding injured soldiers before military vehicles arrived, though access remains a challenge.

title: What IPL teams can and can't do in 2026 edition: BCCI's new guidelines
content: IPL teams will no longer hold practice sessions on match days starting in 2026, with new regulations outlining net and wicket allocations. Strict rules are in place for player movement, fitness tests, and attire during matches and presentations to ensure smooth operations and broadcasting.

title: Odisha man's head shaved, fined Rs 90,000 over inter-tribe marriage
content: A 22-year-old tribal man in Odisha's Koraput district was allegedly forced to pay a Rs 90,000 fine, host a community feast, and have his head publicly shaven after marrying a woman from another tribe. The couple had eloped due to opposition from their respective communities. The traditional settlement ceremony formalized their union after these conditions were met.

title: Watch: Clash at Kolhapur temple after devotees hurl coconuts at priests
content: A brawl erupted at Kolhapur's Jyotiba temple, leading to 17 priests and as many devotees being booked by police. Viral videos showed the clash, causing fear among worshippers. The dispute reportedly began after devotees threw a broken coconut with gulaal at a priest, escalating into a physical altercation with conflicting accounts from both sides.

title: IPL 2026: Can Axar shed 'forever the bridesmaid' tag this season?
content: If Delhi Capitals are to end their IPL title drought, Axar Patel will have to step up with the bat, ball and as a captain. Interestingly, Axar has never crossed the 300-run mark in the IPL in the 162 games he has played over twelve years. The all-rounder is yet to better his debut season with the ball, where he picked up 17 wickets for Kings XI Punjab, now Punjab Kings.

title: Are US-Iran talks happening in Pakistan? What White House said
content: The White House has not confirmed reports of potential meeting between US and Iranian officials in Islamabad, calling the situation sensitive and fluid. Mediating countries, including Pakistan, are reportedly attempting to convene US-Iran talks, though Tehran denies direct negotiations. This diplomatic push follows a pause in US strikes on Iran's energy infrastructure.

title: Bird flu outbreak in Nagpur: 1,000 chickens dead, containment measures on
content: A bird flu outbreak has been confirmed at Nagpur's Central Hatchery following the deaths of nearly 1,000 birds. Authorities are implementing urgent containment measures and have warned of disciplinary action for any negligence. Officials are being directed to comply with orders to prevent further spread of the infection.

title: Man found dead on cot; wife, her lover arrested for murder
content: A 23-year-old woman and a teacher have been arrested for allegedly strangling her husband in Rajasthan's Balotra district. The victim, 21-year-old Mahendra Meghwal, was found dead at his home after a reported argument. Police detained the accused after they confessed to the crime.

title: 'Repeat after me': Jaipur driver held for forcing foreign tourist to abuse in Hindi
content: An auto-rickshaw driver in Jaipur was apprehended by tourist police for tricking a foreign woman into uttering offensive Hindi phrases. The incident, captured on video and widely shared, led to the driver's arrest on charges of misconduct and tourist harassment. Authorities emphasized the deceptive and inappropriate nature of the act.

title: MLB rumors: Braves eye $340M Padres superstar amid outfield woes
content: Atlanta Braves are reportedly eyeing San Diego Padres star Fernando Tatis Jr. ahead of the 2026 season.  A potential trade package includes outfielder Mike Yastrzemski and promising prospects Didier Fuentes, Owen Murphy, and Diego Tornes. This move aims to bolster Atlanta's outfield and lineup, while San Diego could gain valuable young talent and salary relief.

title: Doncic cleared to play: NBA rescinds suspension listing; star available vs Pistons
content: Luka Doncic faces a surprising one-game suspension for the Lakers' March 23rd clash against the Pistons, despite his 16th technical foul being rescinded. This unexpected absence impacts the surging Lakers, who are on a nine-game win streak.  The Pistons, leading the East, will also be without star Cade Cunningham, creating a pivotal matchup.

title: Elliotte Friedman hints at James Hagens’ signing decision coming soon
content: NHL Insider Elliotte Friedman hinted at a decision regarding top prospect James Hagens' professional future by Monday. While Hagens, the Bruins' seventh overall pick in 2025, has impressed, the team might hesitate to rush him into the NHL roster during their playoff push. Friedman believes Hagens is a great fit, but the exact path, NHL or AHL, remains uncertain.

title: IAF civilian employee nabbed for leaking secrets to Pakistan for Rs 3.5 lakh
content: A civilian employee at Assam's Chabua Air Force Station, Sumit Kumar, has been arrested for allegedly passing sensitive information to Pakistan-based handlers for approximately Rs 3.25 lakh.  Investigators are working to uncover the full extent of his involvement and the classified data he leaked, with Kumar remanded in custody until April 2.

title: Fuel frenzy in Gujarat: Thousands rush to petrol pumps across cities amid shortage rumours
content: Panic buying gripped Gujarat as rumours of a fuel shortage led to long queues and temporary rationing at petrol pumps. Dealers cited operational shifts, including advance payment requirements and weekend replenishment gaps, as reasons for temporary disruptions. Officials and oil companies have reassured the public that sufficient fuel stocks are available across the state, urging citizens to avoid unnecessary panic.

title: Beer, tripling, no helmets: 3 Bengaluru doctors booked after video goes viral
content: Three doctors celebrating their Master of Science in Orthopaedics were booked for riding triple on a two-wheeler, with two not wearing helmets. One pillion rider was seen drinking beer amidst traffic. Police registered an FIR for rash and negligent riding after a video of the incident went viral.

title: 'Torso found near canal': Delhi woman beheaded in Bulandshahr; lover among 2 arrested
content: Two men were arrested for allegedly beheading a Delhi woman and dumping her body in Bulandshahr. The prime accused, her live-in partner Vikas Kumar, confessed to the crime, stating he was pressured by the victim to stay with her. He, along with accomplices, decapitated her to conceal her identity.

title: Welcome to WNBA chaos: Breaking down a chock-full offseason
content: the WNBA and women's national basketball players association agree to a new collective bargaining agreement . the agreement gives way to what both sides have described as a "transformational" deal . despite months of negotiations to reach this point, the previous CBA expired in October .

title: What returning production looks like for the 2026 college football season
content: the more continuity and experience you return from last season, the more likely you are to improve . it's still better to return a lot of your production than a little, even if last year's headliner bombed . a combination of injuries, tactical stagnation and disappointing development held back the Tigers .

title: Women's March Madness Day 4 takeaways: How every team reached the Sweet 16
content: the final day of the second round of the 2026 women's NCAA tournament is underway . we're tracking every game as the regional semifinals take shape . the three no. 1 seeds moved on easily, but there was plenty of intrigue elsewhere .

title: Men's Sweet 16 preview: Keys to every team advancing in the NCAA tournament
content: college basketball reporters break down what could decide each game in the next round of the 2026 men's NCAA tournament . the hottest team in the field, winning three games in five days to go from the first four to the Sweet 16 .

title: Ranking the best possible Stanley Cup playoff first-round matchups
content: if things stay the way they are right now, we will have a ton of turnover from last season . the Buffalo Sabres, Pittsburgh Penguins, Columbus Blue Jackets, Boston Bruins, Detroit Red Wings, Anaheim Ducks , Utah Mammoth and Nashville Predators are all ready for the postseason .

title: Wemby states MVP case: Want to leave no debate
content: wembanyama is averaging 24.3 points, 11.2 rebounds, 3.0 assists and 3.0 blocked shots . the San Antonio star wants to win the NBA's MVP award this season . only six players have finished a season with such numbers .

title: Seven stolen dogs escape moving truck in China, travel 17 km together to return to their owners
content: Seven dogs break free from thieves and travel 17 km together to return to their village in China.
